output.title: Neutropenia/CBC interpretation out of scope below 6 months
output.detail: Neutropenia is defined by distinct age-banded ANC cutoffs (<2500x10^9/L in neonates/infants vs <1500x10^9/L in toddlers/older children); applying the older-child cutoff to a neonate or young infant would under-flag neutropenia. This module's supported age range begins at 6 months.
actions: Do not interpret this patient's CBC/ANC result against this module's thresholds — the age-appropriate infant reference range and clinical pathway are out of scope here.
Route to an age-appropriate neonatal/young-infant hematology pathway.

changeRationale: Drafted proposal (P3-T5) joined to dec_cbc_young_infant_scope_abstention_001 — no clinical re-review has occurred; no credentialed clinician has approved this proposal.

output.title: Local ANC reference range required before neutropenia interpretation
output.detail: Pediatric CBC reference intervals are age-banded and analyzer-specific — independently reported CALIPER cohorts found statistically significant age-specific differences for 39 of 47 hematologic parameters, and intervals are not portable across analyzers (e.g. Beckman Coulter DxH 900 vs Mindray BC-6800Plus) without local verification. An ANC value was supplied, but no local/analyzer-specific ANC reference range or local-lab neutropenia flag was provided, so this module cannot resolve neutropenia status and does not fall back to a universal ANC cutoff. Supply a local, analyzer-specific ANC lower reference limit (cbc.localRanges.ancLower) or a local-lab neutropenia flag (cbc.localFlags.neutropenia) before interpreting this ANC result, and confirm the supplied ANC value and any local range use consistent units (x10^9/L vs cells/microliter) — unit mismatches have been documented to distort apparent severity. Do not apply a generic or universal ANC cutoff to this result in place of a verified local range.

changeRationale: Drafted proposal (P4-T2) joined to dec_cbc_local_range_precedence_001 — no clinical re-review has occurred; no credentialed clinician has approved this proposal.

output.title: Multilineage cytopenia — possible marrow failure or infiltrative process
output.detail: Anemia occurring together with another cytopenia (leukopenia, neutropenia, or thrombocytopenia) signals concern for a marrow failure or infiltrative process rather than isolated nutritional anemia. In two independent SCNIR/registry cohorts, persistent congenital neutropenia (ANC persistently below 0.5x10^9/L beyond infancy) carried concentrated myeloid-malignancy (MDS/AML) risk relative to cyclic or autoimmune/idiopathic etiologies — 11.3% versus 4.4% overall in one cohort, and 16% versus 0% (p<0.0001) in a second, independent cohort. This is a referral trigger, not a diagnosis, treatment, or dosing directive.
actions: Obtain prompt pediatric hematology/oncology evaluation for a possible marrow failure or infiltrative process rather than managing as isolated nutritional anemia.
Do not let a co-occurring benign neutropenia differential pattern (e.g. benign-ethnic/Duffy-null neutropenia) suppress or delay this referral.

changeRationale: Drafted proposal (P3-T5) joined to dec_cbc_marrow_red_flag_001 — no clinical re-review has occurred; no credentialed clinician has approved this proposal.

support: Duffy-null phenotype-associated (benign ethnic) neutropenia is the dominant etiology of pediatric neutropenia referrals in this evidence base (77.7% overall; up to 96.6% and 91% in ancestrally relevant referral subgroups).
Absence of recurrent, frequent, or serious infection is the clinician-usable discriminator this evidence names between benign and pathologic neutropenia.
cautions: This is a reviewable pattern, not a confirmed diagnosis — Duffy phenotype/genotype testing and infection history are not yet represented as discrete facts in this module (E1 item).

Never suppress or downrank a co-occurring marrow-red-flag alert (CBC-MARROW-REDFLAG-001 / dec_cbc_marrow_red_flag_001) because this pattern also matched — the red-flag rule must always dominate ranking when both are present.
nextSteps: Confirm absence of recurrent, frequent, or serious infection history.
Consider Duffy-null phenotype/ancestrally relevant context where clinically available.
If any marrow-red-flag feature is present, treat this pattern as non-reassuring and pursue the red-flag referral pathway instead.

changeRationale: Drafted proposal (P3-T5) joined to dec_cbc_benign_neutropenia_differential_pattern_001 — no clinical re-review has occurred; no credentialed clinician has approved this proposal.
